c74object.name: tap.shift~
c74object.description: The tap.shift~ object is an efficient pitch-shifter for audio signals: a phasor sweeps two windowed delay taps across a window of the recent input, transposing it by the given ratio. The algorithm is similar to the well-known harmv~ pitch shifter from IRCAM's Jimmies. The 2026 engine keeps the original's control surface but modernizes the internals: the taps are Hermite-interpolated, the two grain envelopes sum to exactly one (so the output level is steady at any ratio), and ratio and window changes glide over the smooth time instead of stepping. The middle inlet sets the ratio and the right inlet sets the window size, as in the original. For dual-voice shifting with delay, feedback, and modulation, see tap.pitchaccum~.

method.name: float
method.description: In the middle inlet, sets the pitch-shift ratio; in the right inlet, sets the window size in milliseconds.

attribute.name: ratio
attribute.description: The ratio of the desired pitch to the original pitch (1 = no shift, 2 = up an octave, 0.5 = down an octave).

attribute.name: window_size
attribute.description: The size of the sound fragments used in the pitch-shifter, in milliseconds (1..194).

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Interpolation time in ms applied to ratio and window_size changes (0 = immediate; default 20).
